content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.5-xhigh

## Summary

Found two correctness issues in the PR open flow. Verification run: `nix build`, `nix develop -c cargo fmt --check`, `nix develop -c cargo clippy --locked -- -D warnings`, and `nix develop -c cargo test` all completed successfully.

## Amendments

1. src/contexts/bead_workflow/pr_open.rs:297 only checks unstaged and untracked paths before the reset, while src/contexts/bead_workflow/pr_open.rs:496 then runs `git reset --soft origin/master` and src/contexts/bead_workflow/pr_open.rs:523 commits whatever is already in the index. `git reset --soft` preserves any pre-existing staged manual edits, so an operator can have a staged real-code change silently swept into the squashed PR commit even though it was not part of an `rb: checkpoint` commit. Reject or explicitly account for pre-existing staged real-code changes before the soft reset, and add a test with a staged path.
   Classification: fix_current_bead
   Rationale: This defeats the checkpoint-only safety check and can push unrelated manual work in the automated PR commit.
2. src/contexts/bead_workflow/pr_open.rs:332 builds the "real-code" diff stats by excluding only `.ralph-burning/**`, and src/contexts/bead_workflow/pr_open.rs:606 plus src/contexts/bead_workflow/pr_open.rs:615 render that list directly into the PR body and reviewer file list. That treats `.beads/issues.jsonl` and other non-source metadata as reviewer-visible real code, and it can also make src/contexts/bead_workflow/pr_open.rs:510 think there are real-code changes when only backlog metadata changed. Filter the stats/reviewer list to the stated review scope (`src/`, `tests/`, `docs/`, and config files) or explicitly exclude non-code state such as `.beads/`.
   Classification: fix_current_bead
   Rationale: The PR body is supposed to show real-code-only stats and reviewer attention, not backlog/runtime metadata.
